source_line: for (unsigned k = j; k && info[k - 1].cluster == old_cluster; k--)
what_input_feature: A structurally well-formed multi-table font input that repeatedly invokes delete_glyphs_inplace's backward-merge path and reaches the line-633 loop's false exit. Grimoire's generalized token/gap splicing assembles the table-tag and feature structure (e.g. 'typ1', 'feats', 'sn-hans', 'vdsc') that keeps the shaper running long enough to hit the merge loop and exit it.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 9/10 (blocked 1, 6.10h) and its seeds (a732bc59..., d1a42223...) carry recognizable table/script tokens ('typ1', 'feats', 'sn-hans', 'vdsc', 'darfhdscpglh') produced by structural splicing of generalized corpus fragments, building inputs that traverse the cluster-merge code.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog resolves only 2/10 and stays blocked 18.10h; lacking the GeneralizationStage and Grimoire structural mutators it cannot recombine these token/gap structures, so its byte-level I2S substitution does not assemble the deep, valid table structure needed to repeatedly enter the merge loop.
mechanism_attribution: The winning technique is grimoire structural mutation: GeneralizationStage plus the Grimoire splice/recurse mutators build grammar-free structural variants that exercise the cluster-merge path more reliably than cmplog's I2S-only stack. Mechanism is grimoire structural.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid font header where the table-tag/magic token runs ('true', 'loca', 'ttcf') are preserved as generalised structural units while surrounding bytes vary.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire winner seeds repeatedly carry 'true' at 0x00-0x03 (74 72 75 65) plus 'loca' and a 'ttcf' TrueType-collection tag, consistent with GeneralizationStage gapping the variable bytes and Grimoire mutators splicing the preserved token runs to keep the parse path valid.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has no GeneralizationStage or Grimoire structural mutators, so it cannot abstract the magic/tag tokens into reusable structural units and reassemble a valid header; it resolved only 1/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and the Grimoire structural mutators recombine these generalised token/gap structures, letting it rebuild a header that keeps the 'loca'/magic literals intact past the glyf gate.

what_input_feature: The same case-19 driving category run, assembled by recombining generalized token/gap structures rather than discovered by raw byte search. The grimoire winner seeds repeat a small dword token (f2 a8 00 00) and splice it (id=68d1ae222de2c678: f2 a8 00 00 f2 a8 00 00 ...), i.e. a structurally regular category block.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 8/10 (winner) while cmplog resolves 1/10 (loser); winner seeds id=ce09bb2d97eac25b and id=68d1ae222de2c678 are grimoire-discovered, short (14 bytes) and built from a repeated f2 a8 00 00 dword token consistent with GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural splice/recurse rather than scalar mutation.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has the cmplog stack but lacks the GeneralizationStage and the Grimoire structural mutators that grimoire adds; without generalized-token splicing it cannot cheaply build the regular category block that drives state 19, so it stays blocked (loser resolved=1/10 blocked=9).
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage replaces concrete byte runs with <GAP> placeholders and its Grimoire mutators splice/recurse those generalized token structures; this string-based structural recombination assembles the repetitive category-byte block that drives trans-action 19, which the cmplog loser (no generalization, no Grimoire mutators) cannot synthesize.

function: hb-ot-shaper-indic.cc:initial_reordering_syllable_indic(hb_ot_shape_plan_t const*, hb_face_t*, hb_buffer_t*, unsigned int, unsigned int)
what_input_feature: Same indic_symbol_cluster syllable requirement, reached by splicing/recombining generalized token-and-gap structures from the corpus into a layout that decodes to symbol-cluster text.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 8/10 vs cmplog's 1/10; it adds a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators that recombine generalized byte runs, and the grimoire winning seeds show repeated structural motifs (f2 a8 00 00 ... patterns).
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog lacks the generalization + structural mutators (it is grimoire's baseline minus those stages) and stays blocked 9/10 at hitcount 0.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire's added technique over its cmplog baseline is structural mutation: GeneralizationStage replaces concrete runs with gap placeholders and the Grimoire mutators splice/recurse on them, building category-bearing structures that cmplog's byte-local mutators do not assemble.

what_input_feature: A structurally recombined input that places the 0x0E0E/0x0E0F codepoint run inside a generalized token/gap scaffold the Thai shaper parses as a glyph buffer, so the consonant-type gate's true branch is reached.
why_winner_satisfies: grimoire resolves 9/10 trials; its winning seeds (Seed 2 fuzzer=grimoire, Seed 3 fuzzer=grimoire) are larger structured inputs embedding the 0f 0e codepoint run amid spliced token material (e.g. 6d 61 54 ff 0f 0e and ff 0f 0e 00), consistent with Grimoire's structural splice/recurse over generalized inputs.
why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (same baseline plus I2S but WITHOUT the GeneralizationStage and Grimoire structural mutators) blocks 9/10 trials with hitcount 0; it lacks the structural recombination that assembles a buffer reaching the gate.
mechanism_attribution: grimoire and cmplog share the full cmplog/I2S stack; grimoire's only delta is the GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalized token/gap structures (grimoire structural), which is what builds the input reaching the branch.

what_input_feature: A structurally valid font whose planner->props.script resolves to one of the Unicode-7.0+ complex scripts in the case block at lines 331-387 (HB_SCRIPT_GRANTHA and siblings), so dispatch enters the case at 331 and falls through to the shared chosen_script test at 394-398. This requires building OpenType tables (cmap/GSUB/GPOS/GDEF) coherent enough that script detection assigns one of these tags rather than landing in the default arm.
why_winner_satisfies: Grimoire winning seeds carry well-formed sfnt structure (sfnt version 00 01 00 00 at offset 0, table-directory tags like cmap/GDEF/GPOS, OTTO magic) plus filler/region bytes, exactly the deep structural shape grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural mutators recombine; the W side reaches the GRANTHA case (line 331 tagged [B]) and runs the downstream USE shaper (W hits 20 vs L hits 6 on zero_mark_width/adjust_mark_offsets).
why_loser_doesnt: Cmplog loser seeds are short, byte-scrambled fragments (72-218 bytes, ParentInfo havoc/I2S mutation chains like BytesDeleteMutator/ByteNegMutator) whose script resolution stalls at earlier Unicode-3.0..6.1 case labels (lines 278-330 tagged [L]) or the default arm; they never assemble a font that resolves to a Unicode-7.0+ script, so the blocker true side stays unhit (loser blocked 8/10).
mechanism_attribution: grimoire adds GeneralizationStage (replaces byte runs with <GAP> placeholders, validated by re-execution) plus the Grimoire structural mutators that splice/recurse on generalized token/gap structures; this grammar-free structural recombination of whole table fragments builds the coherent multi-table font that drives script detection to a new high-Unicode script tag, whereas cmplog's I2S byte substitution (which grimoire also has) only fixes local CMP operands and cannot synthesize the structural skeleton.
